seo.title: Kansas Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Kansas — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license kansas, dental assistant certification kansas, RDA kansas, DANB CDA kansas, dental assistant radiography kansas

board.name: Kansas Dental Board
board.mailing_address: 900 SW Jackson St, Suite 556, Topeka, KS 66612

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (expires October 1st of even-numbered years)

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (no state license required)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in Kansas.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Visit the Kansas Dental Board Online Services portal (kansas.professions.kcc.ks.gov), create a new user account, and then complete and submit the Expanded Duty Dental Assistant (EDDA) application form online.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload proof of graduation from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program OR documentation of at least 1,000 hours of clinical practice as a dental assistant and completion of a Board-approved EDDA course. Additionally, provide proof of current Basic Life Support (BLS) or CPR certification, and verification of training for each specific expanded duty (e.g., coronal polishing, sealants, nitrous oxide monitoring) you intend to perform.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable $75 application fee directly through the Kansas Dental Board Online Services portal upon submission of your application.

steps.title: Pass Jurisprudence Examination
steps.description: Access and successfully complete the Kansas Jurisprudence Examination, which is typically available through the Kansas Dental Board Online Services portal once your application is submitted.

steps.title: Board Review and Approval
steps.description: The Kansas Dental Board will review your complete application and all submitted documentation. Upon successful verification and approval, your EDDA registration will be issued.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Kansas does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does Kansas require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, Kansas does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Kansas?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Kansas.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Kansas?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Kansas?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Kansas. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.notes: Kansas does not require state licensure for dental assistants. DANB certification recognized.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Kansas
hero.dek: To register as an Expanded Duty Dental Assistant (EDDA) in Kansas, apply through the Kansas Dental Board Online Services portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Kansas Dental Board Online Services portal and click 'Register a new account'. You will be prompted to provide your email address, create a password, and verify your email. Once your account is successfully registered, log in to access the application forms, including the Expanded Duty Dental Assistant (EDDA) application.
